note: Galvanostatic at locked I_amp=0.6 e/fs, one decade lower than the integration test's range. Tests whether the lock-in fits stay clean as we approach the experimentally-relevant frequencies (sim f_W maps to real-cell f via docs/eis_similitude.md).
Same amplitude, decade lower than doe-001. ~1.5 hours estimated. Will tell us whether the |Z| ~ 1/f trajectory continues into the spec ultra-LF region or transitions to a Randles arc.

10x lower amplitude than doe-001, same freq band. Tests the hypothesis that doe-001's R² drop at LF is driven by V_amp leaving linear regime. If doe-003 R²(V) stays > 0.95 across all 4 freqs, hypothesis confirmed and we extend at I=0.06 for the LF arc.
HF extension of integration test range. Looks for the Randles upper asymptote (R_s) where -Im(Z) → 0 and Re(Z) approaches the series-resistance limit. Fast (~3 min).

3x higher amplitude than doe-001 in same freq band. Counterpart to doe-003. We expect this to show even worse R²(V) at LF if the saturation hypothesis is right (reproducing the cycle-3b finding that I=2 gives lower R² than I=0.6 at all freqs).

LF extension at low amplitude — only useful if doe-003 confirms the linearity hypothesis. South can run this opportunistically; if doe-003 fails the hypothesis, we'll queue a STOP and pivot before doe-006 contributes meaningful data.
